title: malus domestica var. paradisiaca
intro: Malus domestica var. paradisiaca, commonly known as the 'Bhagwa' or 'Red Pippin' pomegranate, is a variety of pomegranate native to the Himalayan region. The medicinal properties of this plant include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect cells from damage. The health benefits of consuming Malus domestica var. paradisiaca include lowering cholesterol levels, reducing the risk of heart disease, and acting as an antimicrobial agent to fight off infections. Herbal preparations of Malus domestica var. paradisiaca can be made into teas, extracts, and tinctures, and are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
